page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7nex.large is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. This KVM-hypervised instance features 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 2 dedicated vCPUs. It is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The processor includes an L1 data cache of 48 KB, an L2 cache of 1 MB, and an L3 cache of 48 MB, alongside support for AVX, AVX-512F, AVX-512_VNNI, and cryptographic instruction sets like AES and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile for the ecs.c7nex.large. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the average range. However, multi-core and memory-intensive operations show significant limitations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU score fall into the bottom 10% tier. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only large block and write large block speeds registering in the bottom 10% tier at 14.01 GB/sec and 12.76 GB/sec respectively. Database operations and Redis SET performance are similarly limited, with Redis achieving 1.91 million operations per second, placing it in the bottom 10% performance bracket.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c7nex.large offers dedicated compute resources at a low entry point, making it suitable for workloads that require predictable CPU allocation without the overhead of shared-core resource contention. However, the low memory capacity of 4.0 GB and the lack of local storage present clear resource density tradeoffs. This instance is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, or small-scale utility tasks that do not demand high memory bandwidth or intensive multi-core parallel processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7nex.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of RAM, 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, the instance supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES. Performance benchmarks show average single-core CPU capabilities but lower multi-core and memory bandwidth results, with PassMark and Redis SET operations falling into the lower performance tiers. Qualitatively, this instance provides dedicated CPU allocation at a low resource density, making it a cost-efficient option for lightweight, single-threaded tasks, basic web serving, and utility workloads that do not require high memory capacity or intensive parallel processing.
